req.title: Standing order (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall set up a standing order with start date in the future; past start dates shall be rejected. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests for Standing Order Start Date Validation

```python
"""
Test suite for standing order start date validation.

Requirement: A customer shall set up a standing order with a start date in the
future; past start dates shall be rejected. Boundary behaviour at exact
min/max thresholds must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import date, datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Production code assumed interface (adapt import to your project)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# from banking.standing_orders import StandingOrderService, StandingOrderRequest
# from banking.exceptions import InvalidStartDateError, StandingOrderError

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Minimal stubs so the tests are self-contained and runnable.
# Replace with real imports in your project.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class InvalidStartDateError(ValueError):
    """Raised when a standing order start date is invalid."""


class StandingOrderError(Exception):
    """General standing order error."""


class StandingOrderRequest:
    def __init__(
        self,
        customer_id: str,
        start_date: date,
        amount: float,
        recipient_account: str,
        frequency: str = "MONTHLY",
    ):
        self.customer_id = customer_id
        self.start_date = start_date
        self.amount = amount
        self.recipient_account = recipient_account
        self.frequency = frequency


class StandingOrderResult:
    def __init__(self, order_id: str, start_date: date):
        self.order_id = order_id
        self.start_date = start_date


class StandingOrderService:
    """
    Stub implementation.  Replace with the real service under test.
    """

    MAX_DAYS_IN_FUTURE = 365  # business rule: no more than 1 year ahead

    def __init__(self, clock=None):
        # Injected clock enables deterministic testing.
        self._clock = clock or (lambda: date.today())

    def create_standing_order(self, request: StandingOrderRequest) -> StandingOrderResult:
        today = self._clock()

        if request.start_date < today:
            raise InvalidStartDateError(
                f"Start date {request.start_date} is in the past. "
                f"Today is {today}."
            )

        if request.start_date == today:
            raise InvalidStartDateError(
                f"Start date {request.start_date} must be strictly in the future. "
                f"Same-day standing orders are not permitted."
            )

        max_allowed = today + timedelta(days=self.MAX_DAYS_IN_FUTURE)
        if request.start_date > max_allowed:
            raise InvalidStartDateError(
                f"Start date {request.start_date} exceeds the maximum allowed "
                f"future date of {max_allowed} ({self.MAX_DAYS_IN_FUTURE} days ahead)."
            )

        # Simulate persistence
        return StandingOrderResult(
            order_id="SO-12345",
            start_date=request.start_date,
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

FIXED_TODAY = date(2024, 6, 15)  # Saturday – a deliberate non-trivial date


@pytest.fixture
def fixed_clock():
    """Returns a clock function that always returns FIXED_TODAY."""
    return lambda: FIXED_TODAY


@pytest.fixture
def service(fixed_clock):
    """StandingOrderService with a deterministic clock."""
    return StandingOrderService(clock=fixed_clock)


@pytest.fixture
def valid_request_factory():
    """
    Factory fixture: returns a callable that builds a StandingOrderRequest
    with sensible defaults.  Pass keyword arguments to override any field.
    """
    def _factory(**overrides):
        defaults = dict(
            customer_id="CUST-001",
            start_date=FIXED_TODAY + timedelta(days=1),  # tomorrow – minimum valid
            amount=100.00,
            recipient_account="GB29NWBK60161331926819",
            frequency="MONTHLY",
        )
        defaults.update(overrides)
        return StandingOrderRequest(**defaults)

    return _factory


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def days_from_today(n: int) -> date:
    """Return a date exactly *n* days from FIXED_TODAY (negative = past)."""
    return FIXED_TODAY + timedelta(days=n)


# ===========================================================================
# 1.  BOUNDARY TESTS – Past dates (must be rejected)
# ===========================================================================

class TestPastStartDateRejected:
    """Dates on or before today must raise InvalidStartDateError."""

    def test_yesterday_is_rejected(self, service, valid_request_factory):
        """One day in the past is the closest past boundary."""
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=days_from_today(-1))
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStartDateError, match="past"):
            service.create_standing_order(request)

    def test_today_is_rejected(self, service, valid_request_factory):
        """Today itself is NOT a valid start date (same-day orders disallowed)."""
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=FIXED_TODAY)
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStartDateError):
            service.create_standing_order(request)

    def test_far_past_date_is_rejected(self, service, valid_request_factory):
        """An arbitrarily old date (e.g. 10 years ago) must be rejected."""
        request = valid_request_factory(
            start_date=date(2000, 1, 1)
        )
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStartDateError):
            service.create_standing_order(request)

    def test_start_of_year_in_past_is_rejected(self, service, valid_request_factory):
        """1 January of the current year is in the past relative to FIXED_TODAY."""
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=date(FIXED_TODAY.year, 1, 1))
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStartDateError):
            service.create_standing_order(request)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("days_ago", [2, 7, 30, 90, 364, 365, 730])
    def test_various_past_dates_are_rejected(self, service, valid_request_factory, days_ago):
        """Parametrised: a range of past offsets must all be rejected."""
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=days_from_today(-days_ago))
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStartDateError):
            service.create_standing_order(request)


# ===========================================================================
# 2.  BOUNDARY TESTS – Minimum valid future date (tomorrow)
# ===========================================================================

class TestMinimumValidStartDate:
    """Tomorrow is the earliest permitted start date."""

    def test_tomorrow_is_accepted(self, service, valid_request_factory):
        """Exact lower boundary: today + 1 day must succeed."""
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=days_from_today(1))
        result = service.create_standing_order(request)
        assert result.start_date == days_from_today(1)

    def test_tomorrow_returns_correct_start_date(self, service, valid_request_factory):
        """The returned order carries the requested start date."""
        tomorrow = days_from_today(1)
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=tomorrow)
        result = service.create_standing_order(request)
        assert result.start_date == tomorrow

    def test_tomorrow_returns_order_id(self, service, valid_request_factory):
        """A successfully created order must have an order ID."""
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=days_from_today(1))
        result = service.create_standing_order(request)
        assert result.order_id  # non-empty


# ===========================================================================
# 3.  BOUNDARY TESTS – Maximum valid future date (today + 365 days)
# ===========================================================================

class TestMaximumValidStartDate:
    """Standing orders may not be scheduled more than 365 days in the future."""

    def test_exactly_365_days_ahead_is_accepted(self, service, valid_request_factory):
        """Exact upper boundary must be accepted."""
        request = valid_request_factory(
            start_date=days_from_today(StandingOrderService.MAX_DAYS_IN_FUTURE)
        )
        result = service.create_standing_order(request)
        assert result.start_date == days_from_today(365)

    def test_366_days_ahead_is_rejected(self, service, valid_request_factory):
        """One day beyond the upper boundary must be rejected."""
        request = valid_request_factory(
            start_date=days_from_today(StandingOrderService.MAX_DAYS_IN_FUTURE + 1)
        )
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStartDateError, match="exceeds"):
            service.create_standing_order(request)

    def test_far_future_date_is_rejected(self, service, valid_request_factory):
        """An arbitrary far-future date (5 years) must be rejected."""
        request = valid_request_factory(
            start_date=days_from_today(5 * 365)
        )
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStartDateError):
            service.create_standing_order(request)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("days_ahead", [366, 367, 400, 730, 1000])
    def test_various_far_future_dates_are_rejected(
        self, service, valid_request_factory, days_ahead
    ):
        """Parametrised: multiple over-limit offsets must all be rejected."""
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=days_from_today(days_ahead))
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStartDateError):
            service.create_standing_order(request)


# ===========================================================================
# 4.  VALID DATES WITHIN THE WINDOW
# ===========================================================================

class TestValidDatesWithinWindow:
    """Dates strictly between tomorrow and today+365 must all be accepted."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "days_ahead",
        [1, 2, 7, 14, 30, 60, 90, 180, 364, 365],
    )
    def test_valid_future_dates_accepted(
        self, service, valid_request_factory, days_ahead
    ):
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=days_from_today(days_ahead))
        result = service.create_standing_order(request)
        assert result.start_date == days_from_today(days_ahead)

    def test_next_month_is_accepted(self, service, valid_request_factory):
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=days_from_today(30))
        result = service.create_standing_order(request)
        assert result.order_id is not None

    def test_next_quarter_is_accepted(self, service, valid_request_factory):
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=days_from_today(90))
        result = service.create_standing_order(request)
        assert result.start_date == days_from_today(90)


# ===========================================================================
# 5.  CLOCK INDEPENDENCE – Verify tests are not coupled to wall-clock time
# ===========================================================================

class TestClockIndependence:
    """
    The service must evaluate dates relative to the current date at call time,
    not a hard-coded constant.  We verify this by running the same scenario
    against different injected dates.
    """

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "simulated_today",
        [
            date(2023, 1, 1),   # start of year
            date(2023, 12, 31), # end of year
            date(2024, 2, 29),  # leap day
            date(2099, 12, 31), # far future 'today'
        ],
    )
    def test_tomorrow_is_always_valid_relative_to_clock(
        self, valid_request_factory, simulated_today
    ):
        clock = lambda: simulated_today
        svc = StandingOrderService(clock=clock)
        request = valid_request_factory(
            start_date=simulated_today + timedelta(days=1)
        )
        result = svc.create_standing_order(request)
        assert result.start_date == simulated_today + timedelta(days=1)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "simulated_today",
        [
            date(2023, 1, 1),
            date(2024, 2, 29),
        ],
    )
    def test_today_is_always_invalid_relative_to_clock(
        self, valid_request_factory, simulated_today
    ):
        clock = lambda: simulated_today
        svc = StandingOrderService(clock=clock)
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=simulated_today)
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStartDateError):
            svc.create_standing_order(request)


# ===========================================================================
# 6.  ERROR MESSAGE QUALITY
# ===========================================================================

class TestErrorMessages:
    """Errors must be informative so customers understand the rejection reason."""

    def test_past_date_error_mentions_past(self, service, valid_request_factory):
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=days_from_today(-1))
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStartDateError, match="past"):
            service.create_standing_order(request)

    def test_past_date_error_contains_submitted_date(
        self, service, valid_request_factory
    ):
        bad_date = days_from_today(-5)
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=bad_date)
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStartDateError, match=str(bad_date)):
            service.create_standing_order(request)

    def test_today_error_mentions_same_day_restriction(
        self, service, valid_request_factory
    ):
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=FIXED_TODAY)
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStartDateError, match="future"):
            service.create_standing_order(request)

    def test_too_far_future_error_mentions_exceeds(
        self, service, valid_request_factory
    ):
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=days_from_today(366))
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStartDateError, match="exceeds"):
            service.create_standing_order(request)


# ===========================================================================
# 7.  TYPE SAFETY
# ===========================================================================

class TestTypeSafety:
    """The service must not silently accept wrong types for start_date."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "bad_value",
        [
            "2025-01-01",          # string
            20250101,              # integer
            None,                  # None
            datetime(2025, 1, 1),  # datetime (not date)
        ],
    )
    def test_non_date_start_date_raises(self, service, bad_value):
        """
        Non-date types should raise TypeError or InvalidStartDateError.
        This guards against implicit type coercion hiding bugs.
        """
        with pytest.raises((TypeError, InvalidStartDateError, AttributeError)):
            request = StandingOrderRequest(
                customer_id="CUST-001",
                start_date=bad_value,
                amount=100.00,
                recipient_account="GB29NWBK60161331926819",
            )
            service.create_standing_order(request)


# ===========================================================================
# 8.  CALENDAR EDGE CASES
# ===========================================================================

class TestCalendarEdgeCases:
    """Ensure date arithmetic handles tricky calendar boundaries correctly."""

    def test_year_boundary_crossing_accepted(self, valid_request_factory):
        """A start date crossing a year boundary must be handled correctly."""
        simulated_today = date(2024, 12, 30)
        clock = lambda: simulated_today
        svc = StandingOrderService(clock=clock)
        start_date = date(2025, 1, 15)  # 16 days in future
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=start_date)
        result = svc.create_standing_order(request)
        assert result.start_date == start_date

    def test_leap_day_as_start_date_accepted(self, valid_request_factory):
        """Leap day (Feb 29) as a future start date must be accepted."""
        simulated_today = date(2024, 2, 1)
        clock = lambda: simulated_today
        svc = StandingOrderService(clock=clock)
        start_date = date(2024, 2, 29)  # 28 days in future
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=start_date)
        result = svc.create_standing_order(request)
        assert result.start_date == date(2024, 2, 29)

    def test_end_of_month_start_date_accepted(self, valid_request_factory):
        """Last day of a month as start date must work without date overflow."""
        simulated_today = date(2024, 1, 30)
        clock = lambda: simulated_today
        svc = StandingOrderService(clock=clock)
        start_date = date(2024, 3, 31)
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=start_date)
        result = svc.create_standing_order(request)
        assert result.start_date == start_date

    def test_365_days_on_leap_year_boundary(self, valid_request_factory):
        """
        today + 365 from a date whose 365th day crosses a leap-year Feb 29
        must still be accepted (not accidentally produce 366 days).
        """
        simulated_today = date(2024, 3, 1)   # day after leap day
        clock = lambda: simulated_today
        svc = StandingOrderService(clock=clock)
        max_date = simulated_today + timedelta(days=365)  # 2025-03-01
        request = valid_request_factory(start_date=max_date)
        result = svc.create_standing_order(request)
        assert result.start_date == max_date


# ===========================================================================
# 9.  INTEGRATION-STYLE: Full happy-path scenario
# ===========================================================================

class TestHappyPath:
    """End-to-end scenario verifying a complete valid standing order creation."""

    def test_full_valid_standing_order_creation(self, service):
        """
        A customer provides all required details with a valid future date.
        The returned result must reflect the submitted data.
        """
        start_date = FIXED_TODAY + timedelta(days=10)
        request = StandingOrderRequest(
            customer_id="CUST-999",
            start_date=start_date,
            amount=250.00,
            recipient_account="DE89370400440532013000",
            frequency="MONTHLY",
        )
        result = service.create_standing_order(request)

        assert result is not None
        assert result.order_id, "Order ID must be non-empty"
        assert result.start_date == start_date
```

---

## Design Decisions & Rationale

| Decision | Reason |
|---|---|
| **Injected clock (`lambda: date`)** | Eliminates wall-clock coupling; tests remain green on any calendar date |
| **`FIXED_TODAY = date(2024, 6, 15)`** | Deterministic, non-trivial (mid-year, Saturday); exposes weekend bugs |
| **`valid_request_factory` fixture** | Single source of truth for a valid request; tests only mutate the field under test |
| **Parametrised past/future ranges** | Efficiently covers a spectrum of values beyond the two obvious boundaries |
| **Separate classes per concern** | Clear traceability to requirement clauses; `pytest -v` output reads like a spec |
| **`MAX_DAYS_IN_FUTURE` constant** | Upper boundary test is derived from the constant, so it stays correct if the rule changes |
| **Error message assertions** | Ensures the rejection reason is actionable for the customer, not just a bare exception |
| **Type-safety tests** | Guards against implicit coercion hiding date-comparison bugs at the seam |

"""
Test suite for standing order start date validation.

Requirement: A customer shall set up a standing order with a start date in the
future; past start dates shall be rejected. Boundary behaviour at exact
min/max thresholds must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import date, datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Production code assumed interface (adapt import to your project)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# from banking.standing_orders import StandingOrderService, StandingOrderRequest
# from banking.exceptions import InvalidStartDateError, StandingOrderError

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Minimal stubs so the tests are self-contained and runnable.
# Replace with real imports in your project.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class InvalidStartDateError(ValueError):
    """Raised when a standing order start date is invalid."""


class StandingOrderError(Exception):
    """General standing order error."""


class StandingOrderRequest:
    def __init__(
        self,
        customer_id: str,
        start_date: date,
        amount: float,
        recipient_account: str,
        frequency: str = "MONTHLY",
    ):
        self.customer_id = customer_id
        self.start_date = start_date
        self.amount = amount
        self.recipient_account = recipient_account
        self.frequency = frequency


class StandingOrderResult:
    def __init__(self, order_id: str, start_date: date):
        self.order_id = order_id
        self.start_date = start_date


class StandingOrderService:
    """
    Stub implementation.  Replace with the real service under test.
    """

    MAX_DAYS_IN_FUTURE = 365  # business rule: no more than 1 year ahead

    def __init__(self, clock=None):
        # Injected clock enables deterministic testing.
        self._clock = clock or (lambda: date.today())

    def create_standing_order(self, request: StandingOrderRequest) -> StandingOrderResult:
        today = self._clock()

        if request.start_date < today:
            raise InvalidStartDateError(
                f"Start date {request.start_date} is in the past. "
                f"Today is {today}."
            )

        if request.start_date == today:
            raise InvalidStartDateError(
                f"Start date {request.start_date} must be strictly in the future. "
                f"Same-day standing orders are not permitted."
            )

        max_allowed = today + timedelta(days=self.MAX_DAYS_IN_FUTURE)
        if request.start_date > max_allowed:
            raise InvalidStartDateError(
                f"Start date {request.start_date} exceeds the maximum allowed "
                f"future date of {max_allowed} ({self.MAX_DAYS_IN_FUTURE} days ahead)."
            )

        # Simulate persistence
        return StandingOrderResult(
            order_id="SO-12345",
            start_date=request.start_date,
        )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

FIXED_TODAY = date(2024, 6, 15)  # Saturday – a deliberate non-trivial date


@pytest.fixture
def fixed_clock():
    """Returns a clock function that always returns FIXED_TODAY."""
    return lambda: FIXED_TODAY

def service(fixed_clock):
    """StandingOrderService with a deterministic clock."""
    return StandingOrderService(clock=fixed_clock)


@pytest.fixture
def valid_request_factory():

    Factory fixture: returns a callable that builds a StandingOrderRequest
    with sensible defaults.  Pass keyword arguments to override any field.
    """
    def _fac
